a:T49ab,<div class="specification">
<p>The hearing threshold is how loud a sound needs to be before an individual can perceive the sound. The chart shows the mean value of the hearing threshold for hearing-impaired individuals before and after receiving a cochlear implant. Normal human speech has a range of loudness of from 50 to 60 decibels (dB).</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: This article was published in <em>Brazilian Journal of Otorhinolaryngology</em>, Volume number<br/>81, issue 2, Caixeta Guimarães, A., Machado de Carvalho, G., Duarte, A.S.M., Bianchini, W.A., Bravo Sarasty, A.,<br/>di Gregorio, M.F., Zernotti, M.E., Sartorato, E.L., Menino Castilho, A., Hearing preservation and cochlear implants<br/>according to inner ear approach: multicentric evaluation., pp.190–196 , Copyright Elsevier (2015).]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the effect of the cochlear implant on hearing.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Suggest, with reference to the structure of the ear, a reason that a cochlear implant requires an amplifier.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Describe the normal function of the cochlea.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>b:T474,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. the implant lowered the threshold of hearing<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>allowed quieter sounds to be heard;</p>
<p>b. the person would be able to hear human speech;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>the implant is not connected to the bones (whose function is to amplify sound)<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>the implant bypasses the middle ear<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em> <br/>the amplifier replaces the function of the bones</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. vibrations cause fluid in the cochlea to move;</p>
<p>b. (movement of fluid) causes stimulation/movement of haircells/mechanoreceptors;</p>
<p>c. higher frequency is detected closer to the base of the cochlea / vice versa<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>each frequency stimulates specific hair cells;</p>
<p>d. hair cells generate impulses;</p>
<p>e. connected to the brain by the auditory nerve;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/a/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","a"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.3.SL.TZ0.6"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","a-3-perception-of-stimuli"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Many candidates correctly stated that the cochlear implant lowered the hearing threshold, but fewer stated it would allow human speech to be heard.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>The need for an amplifier as the bones of the middle ear were bypassed was noted by the stronger candidates.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Candidates get confused when describing the function of the cochlea. Most know there is fluid and there are hair cells, but answers were often confused. Only the better candidates stated that hair cells respond to different frequencies of sound.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
